subject: English
title: Sea Turtles (கடல் ஆமைகள்)
summary: This lesson describes the life cycle and challenges of sea turtles, specifically the five species found in India, highlighting the amazing journey of the Olive Ridley turtles.

mcqs.question: How many species of sea turtles are there in the world?
mcqs.answer: There are [[seven]] species of sea turtles worldwide.

mcqs.question: Which are the five species found in Indian coastal waters?
mcqs.answer: The [[Olive Ridley]], the [[Hawksbill]], the [[Green Sea Turtle]], the [[Loggerhead]], and the [[Leatherback]].

mcqs.question: Which is the smallest and most common species in India?
mcqs.answer: The [[Olive Ridley]] is the smallest and most common.

mcqs.question: Which is the largest sea turtle species?
mcqs.answer: The [[Leatherback]] turtle, which grows to a length of [[2.2m]] and weighs up to [[700kg]].

mcqs.question: When do Olive Ridleys come ashore to lay eggs?
How many eggs does a female turtle lay at a time?
mcqs.answer: About [[100 eggs]] in a nest cavity of [[45 cm]] depth.

mcqs.question: How do the eggs hatch?
mcqs.answer: The eggs are incubated by the [[warmth of the sun]].

mcqs.question: What is the incubation period?
mcqs.answer: The eggs hatch after [[45 to 60 days]].
